63acb2f4ea571b63636eed33.leadin: Name these artists who created works entitled Assumption of the Virgin, for 10 points each.
63acb2f4ea571b63636eed33.leadin_sanitized: Name these artists who created works entitled Assumption of the Virgin, for 10 points each.
parts: This artist's Assumption of the Virgin was created for a church in Toledo, where he lived. This artist born on Crete also painted The Disrobing of Christ and The Burial of Count Orgaz.
One work by this artist inspired Manet's Olympia, and this artist's Assumption of the Virgin is the largest altarpiece in Venice, which is also the location of his Pesaro Altarpiece.
This artist's Assumption of the Virgin decorates the dome of a Parma cathedral. This artist depicted Io, Leda, Danae, and Ganymede in four paintings known as the Loves of Jupiter series.
parts_sanitized: This artist's Assumption of the Virgin was created for a church in Toledo, where he lived. This artist born on Crete also painted The Disrobing of Christ and The Burial of Count Orgaz.
One work by this artist inspired Manet's Olympia, and this artist's Assumption of the Virgin is the largest altarpiece in Venice, which is also the location of his Pesaro Altarpiece.
This artist's Assumption of the Virgin decorates the dome of a Parma cathedral. This artist depicted Io, Leda, Danae, and Ganymede in four paintings known as the Loves of Jupiter series.
answers_sanitized: Titian [or Tiziano Vecelli; or Tiziano Vecellio]

63acb2f4ea571b63636eed57.leadin: Answer the following about the African-American soprano Leontyne [LEE-uhn-teen] Price, who is "Michael Jordan famous among people who listen to opera." For 10 points each:
63acb2f4ea571b63636eed57.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about the African-American soprano Leontyne [LEE-uhn-teen] Price, who is "Michael Jordan famous among people who listen to opera." For 10 points each:
parts: Leontyne Price was cast as Bess in a revival of this composer's opera Porgy and Bess. His orchestral works include An American in Paris and Rhapsody in Blue.
Price notably appeared as Aida in 1960 at this Milan opera house, which has also been the site of numerous opera premieres, including Bellini's Norma and Puccini's Madame Butterfly.
Price made her debut with the Met in 1961 as Leonora in this Giuseppe Verdi opera, in which Manrico sings "Di quella pira," and a bunch of gypsies sing the "Anvil Chorus."
parts_sanitized: Leontyne Price was cast as Bess in a revival of this composer's opera Porgy and Bess. His orchestral works include An American in Paris and Rhapsody in Blue.
Price notably appeared as Aida in 1960 at this Milan opera house, which has also been the site of numerous opera premieres, including Bellini's Norma and Puccini's Madame Butterfly.
Price made her debut with the Met in 1961 as Leonora in this Giuseppe Verdi opera, in which Manrico sings "Di quella pira," and a bunch of gypsies sing the "Anvil Chorus."

63acb2f4ea571b63636eed5d.leadin: James Whitcomb Riley is the most prominent figure in this state’s “Golden Age” of literature. For 10 points each:
63acb2f4ea571b63636eed5d.leadin_sanitized: James Whitcomb Riley is the most prominent figure in this state's "Golden Age" of literature. For 10 points each:
parts: Riley, author of the vernacular poem “Raggedy Man,” was known as the “Hoosier Poet” because of his celebrations of this state’s dialect.

Riley’s best known poem was titled for this parentless girl. That poem inspired the much more famous comic strip depictions of this character, who had a dog named Sandy, a benefactor named Daddy Warbucks, and no pupils in her eyes.
Another author associated with Indiana’s Golden Age is this novelist of Sister Carrie and An American Tragedy.
parts_sanitized: Riley, author of the vernacular poem "Raggedy Man," was known as the "Hoosier Poet" because of his celebrations of this state's dialect.

Riley's best known poem was titled for this parentless girl. That poem inspired the much more famous comic strip depictions of this character, who had a dog named Sandy, a benefactor named Daddy Warbucks, and no pupils in her eyes.
Another author associated with Indiana's Golden Age is this novelist of Sister Carrie and An American Tragedy.
answers_sanitized: Little Orphan Annie [or Little Orphant Annie]

63acb2f4ea571b63636eed66.question: For killing this figure, Wealhtheow gives a hero three horses, while in John Gardner’s retelling of this figure’s story, he spares Wealhtheow’s life when her husband won’t protect her. The Nowell Codex describes this figure as a descendant of Cain. Considered a cannibal giant by the denizens of Heorot, his slayer later ventures into this figure’s namesake “mere,” a lake, in which he kills this figure’s unnamed mother. For 10 points, name the first antagonist in Beowulf.

Considered a cannibal giant by the denizens of Heorot, his slayer later ventures into this figure's namesake "mere," a lake, in which he kills this figure's unnamed mother. For 10 points, name the first antagonist in Beowulf.

63acb2f4ea571b63636eee0b.question: In one song titled for this activity, the speaker says his “intention is not to lose what I’ve won,” and that speaker repeatedly addresses “Howard” in that Ben Folds Five song. In another song titled for a practitioner of this sport, the singer says he’s “squandered my resistance/for a pocket full of mumbles, such are promises,” and at the end describes the title athlete saying “I am leaving, I am leaving” though he remains. Bob Dylan’s song protesting the conviction of an athlete in this sport has one man say that the accused is no “Gentleman Jim” and helped muster support to free Rubin Carter. For 10 points Dylan’s “Hurricane” and Warren Zevon’s “Boom Boom Mancini” concern what sport once practiced by Muhammad Ali?
63acb2f4ea571b63636eee0b.question_sanitized: In one song titled for this activity, the speaker says his "intention is not to lose what I've won," and that speaker repeatedly addresses "Howard" in that Ben Folds Five song. In another song titled for a practitioner of this sport, the singer says he's "squandered my resistance/for a pocket full of mumbles, such are promises," and at the end describes the title athlete saying "I am leaving, I am leaving" though he remains. Bob Dylan's song protesting the conviction of an athlete in this sport has one man say that the accused is no "Gentleman Jim" and helped muster support to free Rubin Carter. For 10 points Dylan's "Hurricane" and Warren Zevon's "Boom Boom Mancini" concern what sport once practiced by Muhammad Ali?
